description: Overhaul AssetLoader to replace the placeholder system with the new SpriteGenerator-based sprite system. Create the visual effects system (VisualEffectsManager, Particle, DamageNumber). Integrate sprites into the AnimationManager so that entity SpriteAnimators react to GameEvents. This is the bridge layer between the sprite generation (FEAT-001) and the UI rendering (FEAT-003).
steps: Rewrite src/view/assets/AssetLoader.java: Remove the old placeholder createPlaceholderTexture methods and getPlaceholderTexture. On load(), instantiate SpriteGenerator and generate ALL sprites. Store them in HashMap<String, TextureRegion[]> keyed by entity name + animation state (e.g., 'player_idle', 'goblin_attack'). Add accessor methods: getEntityFrames(String entityName, AnimationState state) -> TextureRegion[], getStatusIcon(StatusType type) -> TextureRegion, getElementIcon(Element element) -> TextureRegion, getMenuFrame() -> TextureRegion, getHpBarFrame() -> TextureRegion, getMpBarFrame() -> TextureRegion, getChestFrames() -> TextureRegion[], getWaveBanner() -> TextureRegion, getBackgroundTile() -> TextureRegion, getParticleTexture(String type) -> TextureRegion. Properly dispose all generated textures in dispose(). Keep the BitmapFont and ShapeRenderer.

Create src/view/effects/Particle.java: Fields: float x, y, velocityX, velocityY, lifetime, maxLifetime, size (1-3 pixels), Color color. Methods: update(float delta) - applies velocity, optional gravity/drift, decreases lifetime. isAlive() -> boolean. getAlpha() -> float (fades as lifetime expires). render(SpriteBatch batch, TextureRegion tex) draws at position with color and alpha.

Create src/view/effects/DamageNumber.java: Fields: float x, y, velocityY, lifetime, alpha, String text, Color color. Constructor takes (String text, float x, float y, Color color). Initial velocityY is upward (~40px/s), decelerates over time. Fades out over ~1 second. Methods: update(float delta), isAlive() -> boolean, render(SpriteBatch batch, BitmapFont font).

Create src/view/effects/VisualEffectsManager.java: Manages lists of active Particle objects and DamageNumber objects. Methods: update(float delta) - updates all particles/numbers, removes dead ones. render(SpriteBatch batch, BitmapFont font, AssetLoader assets) - renders all active effects. spawnDamageNumber(String text, float x, float y, Color color). spawnParticles(String type, float x, float y, int count, Color color) - creates burst of particles at position. spawnSlashEffect(float x, float y). spawnFireEffect(float x, float y). spawnHolyEffect(float x, float y). spawnPoisonEffect(float x, float y). spawnDarkEffect(float x, float y). triggerScreenFlash(Color color, float duration). Methods to handle GameEvent-driven effects: onDamageDealt(GameEvent), onCriticalHit(GameEvent), onSpellCast(GameEvent), onEnemyDeath(float x, float y), onLevelUp().

Enhance src/view/rendering/AnimationManager.java: Add a method getActiveEventType() -> GameEventType (returns current animation's event type or null). Add getActiveEvent() -> GameEvent to expose the full event data for effects integration. This allows the rendering layer to know which visual effects to trigger during an animation.
acceptance_criteria: AssetLoader.load() generates all sprites via SpriteGenerator and stores them in accessor-friendly maps
AssetLoader provides typed accessors for entity frames, status icons, element icons, UI frames, background tiles
AssetLoader.dispose() cleans up all generated textures without leaks
Particle class handles position, velocity, lifetime, fade, and rendering
DamageNumber floats upward, decelerates, fades out over 1 second with color-coded text
VisualEffectsManager spawns particles and damage numbers for different effect types
AnimationManager exposes current event type and event data for integration
verification: Run ./gradlew classes and confirm BUILD SUCCESSFUL with no errors
findings: All files created/modified successfully. AssetLoader now wraps SpriteGenerator with typed accessors. Visual effects system provides particles, damage numbers, and screen flash. AnimationManager exposes getActiveEventType() and getActiveEvent(). EnemyDisplay updated to use new sprite frames instead of removed placeholder textures. BUILD SUCCESSFUL verified.
